Name: Joltik
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It’s soaking up static electricity because it can’t produce a charge on its own.

Name: Galvantula
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by this Pokémon do 50 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon that has an Ability (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [L] Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.

Name: Rowlet
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.

Name: Dartrix
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the United Wings attack.
Flavor-Text: It throws one knifelike feather after another at its enemies, and each one precisely strikes a weak point. These feathers are known as blade quills.

Name: Decidueye
Effects: Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
Discard a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand. If you can’t, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye’s shots never miss.

Name: Tapu Bulu
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It pulls large trees up by the roots and swings them around. It causes vegetation to grow, and then it absorbs energy from the growth.

Name: Houndour
Flavor-Text: It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 100 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

Name: Iron Moth
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Ancient Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: No records exist of this species being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon’s traits match up with an object described in an old book.

Name: Horsea
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

Name: Seadra
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It’s the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.

Name: Kingdra ex
Effects: Put up to 3 [W] Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.
This attack does 50 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: This is a smart and sneaky Pokémon. A pair may work together to steal eggs by having one lure the parents away.

Name: Weavile
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: They travel in groups of four or five, leaving signs for one another on trees and rocks. They bring down their prey with coordinated attacks.

Name: Revavroom ex
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.
Discard this Pokémon and all attached cards.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Drowzee
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

Name: Hypno
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if they attach an Energy card from their hand to the Defending Pokémon, their turn ends.
Flavor-Text: Always holding a pendulum that it swings at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.

Name: Duskull
Effects: Put up to 3 Duskull from your discard pile onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Duskull’s victims suffer an intense chill as the Pokémon siphons their life-force away, glaring at them with its bright red eye all the while.

Name: Dusclops
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 5 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you use this Ability, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: Dusclops’s true form is said to be a bright red eye burning within its body, but no one has ever seen such a thing.

Name: Dusknoir
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 13 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you use this Ability, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It comes and goes between this world and the afterlife. People fear this Pokémon because it is said to suck in lost spirits and take them along.

Name: Cresselia
Effects: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
You may turn 1 of your face-down Prize cards face up. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage. (That Prize card remains face up for the rest of the game.)
Flavor-Text: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Sylveon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. Shuffle that Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Sylveon cuts an elegant figure as it dances lightly around, feelers fluttering, but its piercing moves aim straight for its opponents’ weak spots.

Name: Croagunk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It rarely fights fairly, but that is strictly to ensure survival. It is popular as a mascot.

Name: Toxicroak
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a poison sac at its throat. When it croaks, the stored poison is churned for greater potency.

Name: Bloodmoon Ursaluna
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic [F] Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This special Ursaluna can see in the dark with its left eye and protects itself with mud that is as hard as iron.

Name: Slither Wing
Effects: If your opponent has any Future Pokémon in play, this attack does 120 more damage.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This mysterious Pokémon has some similarities to a creature that an old book introduced as Slither Wing.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.

Name: Golbat
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

Name: Crobat
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you played Janine’s Secret Art from your hand this turn, you may draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.
Flavor-Text: Both of its legs have turned into wings. Without a sound, Crobat flies swiftly toward its prey and sinks its fangs into the nape of its target’s neck.

Name: Absol
Effects: If you have at least 3 [D] Energy in play, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon ex and Pokémon V in play.
Flavor-Text: Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

Name: Inkay
Effects: Look at the top card of your opponent’s deck. You may have your opponent shuffle their deck.
Flavor-Text: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

Name: Malamar
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. If you do, this attack does 120 damage to the new Active Pokémon. If you didn’t play Xerosic’s Machinations from your hand during this turn, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar’s control.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When this legendary Pokémon’s wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of living creatures.

Name: Okidogi ex
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [D] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, this Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If this Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 130 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Munkidori ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, and if you have any Pecharunt ex in play, your opponent takes 1 fewer Prize card.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Dirty Headbutt.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Fezandipiti ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, you may draw 3 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Flip the Script Ability each turn.
Effects: This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pecharunt ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch 1 of your Benched [D] Pokémon, except any Pecharunt ex, with your Active Pokémon. If you do, the new Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. You can’t use more than 1 Subjugating Chains Ability each turn.
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Genesect
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, your opponent can’t play any ACE SPEC cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: This ancient bug Pokémon was altered by Team Plasma. They upgraded the cannon on its back.

Name: Cufant
Flavor-Text: Cufant can lift loads weighing five tons. In the mornings, it heads into caves with its herd, in search of the ore on which these Pokémon feed.

Name: Copperajah
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t play any Stadium cards from their hand.
Effects: You may do 100 more damage. If you do, during your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Copperajah are prideful, cantankerous Pokémon. Specimens with vibrant green skin command the respect of others of their kind.

Name: Varoom
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The steel section is Varoom’s actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

Name: Axew
Flavor-Text: If you see peculiar teeth marks on boulders or trees, it means an Axew is likely living nearby.

Name: Fraxure
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Fraxure uses its thick tusks to neatly cut prey into two portions—one to eat now and one to save for later.

Name: Haxorus
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, it is Knocked Out.
Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It overwhelms enemies with its prized tusks, which are sharp enough to cut through a metal transmission tower in one strike.

Name: Kyurem
Ability-Effect: If your opponent has any cards in their discard pile that have “Colress” in the name, this Pokémon can use the Trifrost attack for [C].
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 110 damage to 3 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Dwelling within it is a power even greater than that of Reshiram or Zekrom, but the extreme cold keeps that power bound.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It loves things that sparkle. When it sees a shiny object, the gold coin on its head shines, too.

Name: Persian
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It has a vicious temperament. Beware if it raises its tail straight up. This is a signal that it is about to pounce and bite.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards of different types, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Furfrou
Effects: Attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

Name: Stufful
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.

Name: Bewear
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.

Name: Academy at Night
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may put a card from their hand on top of their deck.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Binding Mochi
Description: Attacks used by the Poisoned Pokémon this card is attached to do 40 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Cassiopeia
Description: You can use this card only when it is the last card in your hand.Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Colress’s Tenacity
Description: Search your deck for a Stadium card and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Dangerous Laser
Description: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Janine’s Secret Art
Description: Choose up to 2 of your [D] Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic [D] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to your Active Pokémon in this way, it is now Poisoned.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Neutralization Zone
Description: Prevent all damage done to Pokémon that don’t have a Rule Box (both yours and your opponent’s) by attacks from the opponent’s Pokémon ex and Pokémon V. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)This card can’t be put into your hand or deck from the discard pile.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Night Stretcher
Description: Put a Pokémon or a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Poké Vital A
Description: Heal 150 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.This card can’t be put into your hand or deck from the discard pile.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Powerglass
Description: At the end of your turn (after your attack), if the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to it.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Xerosic’s Machinations
Description: Your opponent discards cards from their hand until they have 3 cards in their hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Tapu Bulu
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It pulls large trees up by the roots and swings them around. It causes vegetation to grow, and then it absorbs energy from the growth.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 100 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

Effects: Put up to 3 Duskull from your discard pile onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Duskull’s victims suffer an intense chill as the Pokémon siphons their life-force away, glaring at them with its bright red eye all the while.

Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 5 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you use this Ability, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: Dusclops’s true form is said to be a bright red eye burning within its body, but no one has ever seen such a thing.

Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It comes and goes between this world and the afterlife. People fear this Pokémon because it is said to suck in lost spirits and take them along.

Effects: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
You may turn 1 of your face-down Prize cards face up. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage. (That Prize card remains face up for the rest of the game.)
Flavor-Text: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Munkidori
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached, you may move up to 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Munkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.

Name: Fezandipiti
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached and is damaged by an attack, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Fezandipiti beats its glossy wings to scatter pheromones that captivate people and Pokémon.

Name: Okidogi
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached, it gets +100 HP, and the attacks it uses do 100 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Okidogi is a ruffian with a short temper. It can pulverize anything by swinging around the chain on its neck.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

Flavor-Text: Fraxure uses its thick tusks to neatly cut prey into two portions—one to eat now and one to save for later.

Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Earthen Vessel
Description: You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Powerglass
Description: At the end of your turn (after your attack), if the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to it.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
